originalTitle: US sanctions Rwanda's military and top commanders over fighting in DR Congo
title: US sanctions Rwanda's military and top commanders over fighting in DR Congo
content: Rwanda accused Washington's move as “one sided” and politically motivated.

originalTitle: Anglican divisions deepen as rebel clerics pick rival to first female leader
title: Big News in Our Church Family!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends in Africa! I'm your super kid-friendly news reporter, and I have some big news to share today about a very important church family. Imagine a huge family, with brothers, sisters, cousins, aunts, and uncles all over the world. That's a bit like the Anglican Church. It's a very big church family with members in many, many countries, including right here in Africa! This big family has a special leader, like the head of the family or the principal of a super big school. This leader is called the Archbishop of Canterbury. It's a very important job! For a very, very long time, all the leaders in this big church family were men. But guess what? Something new and exciting happened recently!

A Special New Leader!

For the first time ever, a *woman* has been chosen to be a very important leader in this big Anglican church family! She is called the Archbishop of Canterbury. This is a huge step for many people! It means that women can also have very important roles and lead many churches. Many people are very happy about this. They think it's wonderful that both men and women can be leaders in the church, sharing their wisdom and love. **Did you know?** The Anglican Church started many, many years ago in a country called England. But it grew so big that it now has churches in almost every country in the world!

Having Different Ideas

Now, just like in any big family, not everyone always agrees on everything. Sometimes, brothers and sisters have different ideas, right? Well, some of the church leaders, especially those who like to keep things the way they have always been, have different ideas about a woman being such a big leader. These leaders are sometimes called "conservative clerics." "Conservative" means they like to follow old traditions and rules very closely. "Clerics" are just a fancy word for church leaders, like pastors or priests. They believe that, according to their old traditions, only men should be the very top leaders in the church.

A Meeting in Our Africa!

Because they have these different ideas, some of these conservative church leaders decided to have a special meeting. And guess where this important meeting is happening? Right here in Africa! In a big and beautiful country called Nigeria! This meeting is very important because these leaders are planning to choose their *own* special leader. They want to choose someone different from the new woman leader. This new leader would be for *their* group of churches that follow the older traditions. This new leader is sometimes called a "rival" leader. It's a bit like having two different teams in a game. Both teams are part of the same big club, but they have different captains because they like to play in slightly different ways. **Did you know?** Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means many, many people live there. It's a very important place!

Why Are They Doing This?

These church leaders meeting in Nigeria feel very strongly about their beliefs. They love God and their church very much. They believe that by choosing their own leader, they can make sure their churches continue to follow the old ways that they feel are right. It's not about being mean or angry. It's about people having different ways of understanding their faith and traditions. Imagine if you and your friend both love drawing, but you like to draw animals and your friend likes to draw houses. You both love drawing, but you have different styles!

What Does This Mean For Our Churches?

You might be wondering, "What does this mean for *my* church? Or for the churches in Africa?" Well, even though there are different ideas, all these churches are still part of the big Anglican family. They all love God and want to help people. They will continue to have services, sing songs, and teach about kindness and love. This news just means that sometimes big families have big discussions and try to figure out the best way forward for everyone. **Did you know?** There are millions of Anglican Christians in Africa. The church is growing very fast here!

Let's Talk About Understanding

It's very important to remember that even when people have different ideas, we should always try to be kind and understand each other. Some people are very happy about the new woman leader, and some people feel it's better to stick to older ways. Both groups care deeply about their church. Just like in your classroom, you might have different opinions with your friends, but you still play together and respect each other. This is a time for people in the church family to talk, listen, and try to find ways to respect everyone's beliefs, even if they don't agree on everything.

Our African Voice is Important!

The fact that this important meeting is happening in Nigeria shows how important Africa is in the big Anglican family. African church leaders have a very strong voice and play a big role in deciding how the church moves forward. They are sharing their wisdom and helping to shape the future of this global church family. **Did you know?** Many new churches are being built in Africa because so many people are joining the Christian faith!

Keeping Our Hearts Open

So, dear friends, remember this big news: a woman is now a very important leader in the big Anglican church family, and some other leaders are meeting in Nigeria to choose their own leader because they have different ideas. It's a story about different ideas, respect, and how a big family learns to live together, even with disagreements. Let's keep our hearts open and pray for all the leaders, both men and women, as they work hard to guide their church family with love and faith. The church is about loving God and loving our neighbours, and that will always stay the same, no matter who the leaders are or where the meetings happen! Stay tuned for more updates from your super news reporter!
summary: Some church leaders in Africa are choosing a different leader because they have different ideas about a new woman leader in a big church family.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that even big church families can have different ideas, and it helps us understand how different people lead churches around the world. It also shows how important African leaders are in these big decisions.

title: Death toll in 'surprise' attack in South Sudan rises to 178, local official says
content: Medical aid groups have described the scale of casualties as alarming as people seek treatment.

originalTitle: ‘Russian oil will be sought’: What are Moscow’s gains from the war in Iran?
title: Friends, Oil, and Grown-Up Talks!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa, ready to share some cool stories from around the world. Today, we're going on a little adventure to learn about two big countries very far away. Imagine looking at a giant map of the world. Can you see a really, really big country that stretches across so much land? That country is called Russia! And a bit closer, we'll find another important country named Iran. We're going to talk about how these countries are friends, and something special called 'oil,' and how grown-ups talk to each other to solve big puzzles. Are you ready? Let's go!

Meet Russia! A Giant Land!

Imagine a country so, so big that it takes many, many hours to fly from one side to the other in an aeroplane! That's Russia. It's the biggest country in the whole wide world! Russia has lots of forests, big snowy mountains, and even big cities with tall buildings. Many people live there, and they speak a language called Russian. Think of it like a super-duper-sized playground that goes on and on. Did you know? Russia is so big that when the sun is setting in one part of Russia, it's already morning in another part! It's like having different times of day all in one country!

Meet Iran! A Land of History!

Now, let's look at another country, Iran. Iran is also a big and beautiful country, not as big as Russia, but still quite large! It's located in a part of the world called the Middle East. Iran has a very long and exciting history, with ancient stories and amazing old buildings. People there speak a language called Farsi. Imagine a place with lots of sunshine and interesting traditions. Did you know? Some of the oldest cities in the world are found in Iran, with buildings and stories that are thousands of years old!

What Are "Friend Countries" or Allies?

Just like you have best friends at school or in your neighbourhood, countries can have best friends too! When countries are very good friends, we call them 'allies.' Allies like to help each other, share ideas, and work together on important things. Russia and Iran are like good friends on the world map. They talk to each other a lot, and they try to help each other when they need to. It's like having a special teammate in a big game. Did you know? Countries become allies because they have similar goals or want to help each other stay safe and strong!

What is Oil and Why is it Special?

Have you ever wondered what makes a car go vroom? Or how we get electricity to light up our homes and schools? A very important part of that is something called 'oil'! Oil is a thick, dark liquid that comes from deep, deep under the ground. It's like a special treasure that we dig up. People turn this oil into fuel for cars, buses, and aeroplanes. It also helps make electricity so our lights can shine and our phones can work. Oil is super important because almost everything we use needs energy, and a lot of that energy comes from oil. Imagine if we didn't have oil – cars wouldn't move, and many lights might go out! Both Russia and Iran have lots of oil under their land. Did you know? Oil is sometimes called 'black gold' because it's so valuable and important for the world!

When Grown-Ups Talk About Big Changes

Sometimes, grown-ups talk about 'war.' War means big, big fights between countries. It's not nice, and everyone hopes these fights stop very quickly so people can be safe and happy. But when there are big fights or changes in the world, things can change for everyone, even for countries that are not fighting directly. It's like when there's a big storm; even if it doesn't hit your house, it might change how you travel or what you do that day.

How Russia Can Help Its Friend and Get Something Good

Because Russia and Iran are friends (allies), and Russia has a lot of oil, something interesting can happen when there are big changes in the world. Imagine your friend needs a special toy, and you have many of them. You can share, right? Well, if other countries need more oil because of big changes, they might look for places to buy it. Russia has lots of oil that it can sell. When Russia sells its oil, it gets money. This money is called 'profit' or 'gains.' It's like when you help your parents with chores and they give you a little treat! Russia can use this money to build new schools for children, make better roads, or help its people in many ways. So, if other countries need more oil, Russia might sell more and get good things in return. This is one way a country can 'profit' from world changes, even if it's not directly involved in fights. Did you know? The money a country makes from selling its resources, like oil, helps it grow and develop, bringing good things to its people!

Talking to Lots of Friends

Now, here's another interesting part of this big grown-up puzzle. While Russia is good friends with Iran, Russia also talks to another country called Israel. Israel is in the same part of the world as Iran, but sometimes these two countries don't always agree. It's like how you can have different friends in different classes at school. Russia tries to be friendly and talk nicely to both Iran (its close friend) and Israel (another important country). This is called 'diplomatic relations' – it's a big word for countries talking to each other, listening, and trying to find ways to get along or help solve problems. It's like being a good listener and a good talker, even when things are complicated. Did you know? Countries have special people called 'diplomats' who are experts at talking to leaders from other countries and making plans!

It's a Big Grown-Up Puzzle!

So, my bright young friends, you've learned a lot today! We talked about Russia, a giant country, and Iran, a country with lots of history. We learned that countries can be friends, called 'allies,' and that a special liquid called 'oil' is super important. We also saw how Russia might sell more of its oil and get good things when there are big changes in the world, and how it talks to different countries like Iran and Israel. All these things – countries being friends, selling oil, and talking to everyone – is like a very big and important puzzle that grown-ups work on every day. But it's good for us to learn a little bit about how the world works, isn't it? Keep being curious!
summary: Russia, a very large country, and Iran, a country with a rich history, are like good friends who help each other, and Russia might gain good things, like selling more of its special 'oil' and getting money, during big changes in the world, while also talking to other countries like Israel.
whyMatters: This story is important because it helps us understand how different countries work together and how important things like oil are for the world, even when grown-ups are trying to solve big, tricky problems.

originalTitle: ‘US president has the authority to act for imminent threats against the US’
title: America's Leader: Ready for Anything!
content:
Jambo, wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from faraway lands! Today, we're going to chat about something important happening in a big country called America. It's really far from our beautiful Africa, but it's good to know about how leaders around the world work to keep everyone safe and sound.

Meet the Leader of America!

Imagine your school has a principal, or your village has a chief. This person is very important, right? They help make sure everyone is safe and happy, and that things run smoothly. Well, a big country like America also has a very important leader! We call this leader the 'President'. The President of America is like the head of a very big family. Their main job is to look after all the people in America. That means making sure they have good schools, safe roads, and that everyone can live happily. It's a huge job, just like a chief caring for a whole village or a parent looking after all their children. The President works very hard every day. They meet with many people and make big decisions. Their job is to keep America strong and peaceful. It's a lot of responsibility, but it's also a very special role! Did you know? The President of America lives in a very famous house called the White House! It has lots of rooms and a big garden. It's where they do a lot of their important work for the country.

What is 'Having Authority'?

Now, our news today talks about the President having 'authority'. That's a big word, but it just means having the 'power' or 'permission' to do special things. Think about it like this: your teacher has the authority to make sure everyone in the class is learning and behaving. Your parents have the authority to decide what's best for you at home. The President of America has special authority because the people of America chose them to lead. This means they have the power to make important decisions for the country. It's like being given a special tool to do a very important job. You need the right tool to build something strong, right? The President's authority is like that special tool. They don't just get to do anything they want. Their authority comes with rules and responsibilities, just like your teacher has rules to follow when they teach you. It's about using power wisely to help everyone. Did you know? 'Authority' means you are trusted to make big decisions and people will listen to you because it's part of your job! It's about leadership and responsibility.

What are 'Threats'? And 'Imminent' Ones?

Our news also talks about 'imminent threats'. Let's break that down! A 'threat' is like a problem or a danger that could happen. Imagine if a big storm was coming, or if there was something that could make people sick. Those would be threats to our safety and happiness. Now, 'imminent' means something is going to happen *very, very soon*. It's not something that might happen next year; it's something that could happen today or tomorrow! Like when you see dark clouds gathering and you know it's going to rain any minute – that rain is imminent. So, an 'imminent threat' means a *big problem* or *danger* that is about to happen *very, very soon* to America. It's not just a small problem; it's something that could really hurt people or cause big trouble for the country. The President needs to be ready for these things. It could be anything from a very dangerous situation inside the country to a problem coming from far away. The important thing is that it's a danger that needs quick action to stop it from getting worse. Did you know? In nature, animals sometimes sense 'imminent threats' like big storms or even earthquakes before humans do! They get restless and try to find a safe place.

Acting Fast to Keep People Safe!

So, when the news says the President has the authority to 'act' for imminent threats, it means they have the special power to *do something quickly* to stop the bad thing from happening. It's like a superhero who sees danger coming and has to use their powers right away to save the day! If a big danger is coming very, very soon, the President doesn't have time to wait. They have to make quick decisions to protect the people of America. This might mean telling brave soldiers what to do, or talking to other world leaders to find a solution. Their job is to keep everyone safe and sound. Imagine your friend is about to fall, and you quickly reach out to catch them. That's acting fast to stop something bad! The President's job is even bigger – protecting a whole country from something very serious that is about to happen. They need to be brave and smart to make the best choices for their country. This special power means they can move fast when time is short to avoid a big problem. Did you know? Leaders of countries often have special teams of experts who help them understand dangers and make good decisions quickly. It's like having a team of wise helpers!

What This Means for Us in Africa

You might be thinking, 'Why is this important for us, all the way in Africa?' That's a great question, my young friends! Even though America is far away, the whole world is like one big village. What happens in one part of the world can sometimes affect other parts. When a big country like America is safe and peaceful, it helps to make the whole world a bit safer and more peaceful too. Also, this news reminds us that leaders everywhere have an important job. Just like the President of America, our own wonderful leaders here in Africa – our presidents, our chiefs, our community elders – also work hard every day to keep *us* safe and happy! They make sure our schools are good, our water is clean, and our homes are peaceful. They also have the authority to make decisions to protect our countries from problems. It's a big responsibility, and they do it because they care about all of us, the children and families of Africa. Every good leader's job is to protect their people. Did you know? There are 54 different countries in Africa, and each one has its own special leader, like a president or a king, working to protect its people! That's a lot of leaders working hard!

Working Together for a Safe World

So, in simple words, the President of America has a very important job. When there's a big, close-by danger to America, they have the special power to act quickly to keep everyone safe. It's like having a superhero on duty for a whole country! This shows us that leaders around the world are always thinking about how to protect their people. And when leaders work together, even from far away, it helps make our big, beautiful world a safer place for all of us, no matter where we live – from the bustling cities of America to our sunny villages right here in Africa. We all want to live in a peaceful and safe world where children can play and learn without worry. Knowing that leaders are working to keep their countries safe is a part of that peace. It helps us feel more secure. Let's all hope for peace and safety everywhere. That's all for today's news! See you next time, my amazing African friends! Did you know? The United Nations is like a big club for almost all the countries in the world where leaders meet to talk about peace and safety for everyone!
summary: The President of America has special power to act very quickly to keep the country safe from dangers that are about to happen.
whyMatters: It's important to know that leaders everywhere, like our own in Africa, work hard to protect their people and keep our world safe and peaceful.
